Checkup app (from Mobile Klinik; a Canadian store for buying, selling, and repairing smartphones)
Mainly used to run device-health diagnostics, estimate device value post-diagnostics (for re-selling at a Mobile Klinik location), and finding nearby Mobile Klinik locations to book appointments for device repair. Also contains ads and promotions for new devices and accessories.
Generally regarded as bloatware because diagnostics are generic at best, and is eager to request many unnecessary and potentially invasive device permissions.
Safe to remove if you don't use it, and can be re-downloaded from the Google Play Store at any time.

O2 & Telefonica carrier bloat. Runs in background, updates, maintains and installs Telefonica carrier apps and services, and is totally useless if you don't use this carrier.
O2 and Telefonica are only available in Latin America, Brazil, Spain, Germany and UK, but it's often preinstalled on EU, Global and possibly other ROMs, as countries where this carrier is present use these ROMs. Safe to delete.
Presumably works together with com.xiaomi.entitlement.o2. It's also a package safe to remove if you don't use the carriers mentioned earlier.
Provides an Out Of The Box experience. You can disable it via Settings > Apps > Three dots > Show system > App selector. It has a T-Mobile icon

AppCloud
Seems to exist to try to get you to download games and another testimonial.

Aura from Unity
Persistent notification until you click on it and agree to install games. Sort of game cloud pre-installed in some docomo carrier phones
Safe to remove.

Aura from Unity
Persistent notification until you click on it and agree to install games. Sort of game cloud pre-installed in some SoftBank carrier phones
Safe to remove.

Aura from Unity
Persistent notification until you click on it and agree to install games. Sort of game cloud pre-installed in some Rakuten Mobile carrier phones
Safe to remove.
Likely a placeholder for docomo's corporate MDM service. Empty package and disabled by default on consumer devices.

Anshin-security (https://www.docomo.ne.jp/service/anshin_security/)
docomo's security suite providing virus scanning, spam call/email prevention and so on.
Safe to remove, but you may want to check https://mydocomo.docomo.ne.jp for subscriptions. Frequently bundled with device insurance service.

APN switching control
Resource-only stub. Safe to remove.
May potentially cause tethering issues on legacy (pre-2016) models due to docomo's APN-switching requirements?
Backup feature for application of docomo's service, mainly provides schedule/memo's cloud sync function.

Recommended usage tips
Intrusive. Provide "helpful hints" based on user activity, but behaving like adware by showing frequent pop-ups.
Requires high-level permissions such as Accessibility and "Draw over other apps" (overlay) to monitor user actions.
It's not enabled by default, but uninstalling recommended for a cleaner, more private experience.

d-menu
Web page shortcut and notification receiver.

docomo service/cloud Settings
Adds a "docomo service/cloud" entry to the system Settings app. Simple navigation screen providing entry points to docomo-specific app settings, such as d-account and docomo application manager.
Essential for launching d-account settings.
Safe to remove if you don't use any docomo service.

docomo LIVE UX
docomo's default home screen (launcher).
Safe to remove ONLY IF another launcher is installed. Certain devices such as dtab may not have any other home screen apps preinstalled.
docomo application manager (com.nttdocomo.android.applicationmanager) must be installed to use this.

d ACCOUNT Settings
Account Settings for d-account, includes an authentication component for other docomo apps.
Safe to remove if you don't use any docomo services, but ensure you check your login method as you may need this app for two-step authentication when logging in from other devices.

my daiz (https://www.docomo.ne.jp/service/mydaiz/)
docomo's information (e.g. weather, news) app. Also displays a mascot (looks like tofu) on docomo LIVE UX. Requires lots of permissions.

+Message
A messaging app using a carrier-customized RCS implementation by Softbank, au, and docomo. It adds LINE-like features to the standard SMS/RCS.
Safe to remove, but recommended to have an alternative SMS app for OTPs.
Google Messages is a simpler and more standard alternative.

SmartNews for docomo
News app. Special version of SmartNews for docomo user.

Office Link Settings
Provides "Office Link" service (docomo's enterprise internal line service). Typically accessed through the settings menu of the OEM Phone app.
Safe to remove.

A background platform service that manages docomo's proprietary IP push notification system.
Safe to remove if you don't need to receive docomo's service notifications.

Omakase-lock (https://www.docomo.ne.jp/service/lock/)
Remote device lock service. Allows docomo customer support to lock your device upon request.
Safe to remove. Google's "Find Hub" are better-integrated alternatives including NFC (=FeliCa) remote lock.
NOTE: To uninstall this, open settings > App permission > Special app access > Device admin apps and disable this app (May vary depending on your device).
For FeliCa remote lock, a separate package (com.nttdocomo.android.felicaremotelock) is used.

Schedule & Memo
2 different apps in one package; docomo's schedule and memo apps.

Remote Wipe (https://www.docomo.ne.jp/service/initialization/)
Allows docomo customer support to wipe your device upon request. This does NOT clear data inside the FeliCa chip.
Safe to remove. Google's "Find Hub" or oem provided services are better-integrated alternatives.
NOTE: To uninstall this, open settings > App permission > Special app access > Device admin apps and disable this app (May vary depending on your device).

Anshin-Filter for docomo (https://www.docomo.ne.jp/service/anshin_filter/0)
Carrier-provided parental controls. Terrible.
Causes excessive battery drain on both parent and child devices due to heavy background polling, and a ton of other issues.
If you care about your child's better well-being, digital experience and parent-child relationships, consider using alternative services such as Google Family link.
Strongly recommended to uninstall.

docomo mail
Carrier mail app. Safe to remove.

docomo location information
Provides "Keitai-Osagashi Service" (Mobile Phone Finder Service) and "imadoco search" (parental tracking) by docomo.
com.nttdocomo.android.docomoset is needed to launch settings screen.
Safe to remove, but you may want to check https://mydocomo.docomo.ne.jp for subscriptions and "imadoco search" Opt-Out at https://spf.spmode.ne.jp/location/posinfo_sp (spf.spmode.ne.jp is only accessible via mobile network).
Google's "Find Hub" and "Family Link" are free and better alternatives integrated with Android.

Disaster kit (https://www.docomo.ne.jp/info/disaster/disaster_kit/)
A dedicated app for docomo's safety registration service used during major emergencies. It allows users to post and search for safety status.
NOTE: This is not related to the core functionality of Area Mail (early warning). For checking Area Mail history, you can find it in system settings.
Safe to remove; use web171.jp, Disaster Message Dial (171) or LINE's safety status feature instead for about safety status.
The unlocking interface and process when unlocking a Tracfone-based phone. Probably needed for unlocking your carrier status. probably needed if you want to unlock your phone from a Tracfone service.
